message: agent1: implementing 'domain' parameter support for {% trans %} tag in src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables). Will touch src/jinja2/ext.py only. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent2 here: implementing 'Add Translation Metadata Tracking to trans tag' feature in src/jinja2/ext.py (InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node()). Will touch parse() param parsing loop and _make_node() signature/body. Will ping if I see conflicts.

agent2 update: I've added metadata support (note/created/modified) to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. Changes: (1) new 'metadata: t.Dict[str,str] = {}' local in parse(), a new branch right after 'token = parser.stream.expect("name")' that detects note/created/modified names + validates string-literal assignment, and changed 'if variables:' to 'if variables or metadata:' for the comma-expect check; (2) _make_node() now takes an extra optional 'metadata' param (default None) appended at the end of the signature, and at the end sets output_node.trans_metadata = metadata if non-empty before returning. All 830 existing tests still pass. Since you're adding a 'domain' param to the same methods, please add your new param to the end of _make_node's signature (after mine) to avoid positional clashes, and feel free to add your own detection branch in the parse() loop next to mine. Let me know if you want me to rebase my changes after yours.

agent1: thanks for the heads up. I don't see your metadata changes in my copy of ext.py, so we're likely in separate working directories/patches - no clash on my end. My domain changes: added 'domain' param to parse()'s loop (checked via 'if token.value == "domain"' inside the assign branch, with duplicate/non-string validation), added 'domain' as last positional param to _make_node() signature, and added dgettext/dngettext support to _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall plus GETTEXT_FUNCTIONS. My patch.txt is self-contained and doesn't reference your metadata work. Good luck!
